mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month momentum in real activity-sensitive retail sales indicates resilient household demand and rising expected revenues, so stronger RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15 the latest RSAFS vintage was 2022-06 with value 680591.0
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tercile tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is accelerating while the federal funds rate is rising, markets price a faster tightening path and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 6% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 50bp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15 CPIAUCSL vintage for 2022-06 was 295.328 and FEDFUNDS was 1.21
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than the unconditional base rate, or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS change has a non-negative coefficient in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor income and recession risk, so a material upward move in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15 the latest UNRATE vintage was 2022-06 with value 3.6
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not lower after UNRATE increases greater than 0.3pp over 3 months than after stable or falling UNRATE, using walk-forward tests with p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future pressure on construction, durables, and credit-sensitive earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 12-month moving average while FEDFUNDS is above its 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15 the latest HOUST vintage was 2022-05 with value 1549.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-start weakness does not underperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns by at least 1 percentage point on average, or if the HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production momentum indicates improving output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, so stronger INDPRO growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15 the latest INDPRO vintage was 2022-06 with value 104.3648
failure_condition: Reject if months in the top tercile of 3-month INDPRO growth do not have higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than months in the bottom tercile, or if walk-forward Rank IC is persistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate tighten financial conditions and raise equity discount rates before earnings adjust, so large short-run FEDFUNDS increases predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 75bp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15 the latest FEDFUNDS vintage was 2022-06 with value 1.21
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day S&P 500 returns following FEDFUNDS increases greater than 75bp over 3 months are not below unconditional 5-day returns, or if the signal hit rate is below 50% for negative returns in triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with low unemployment signals durable household income and low near-term recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation pressure is not the dominant shock.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change is positive and UNRATE is below 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15 PAYEMS was 151980.0 and UNRATE was 3.6 for the latest 2022-06 vintage
regime_hint: labor-resilient expansion excluding inflation shock dominance
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction is not associated with higher 20-day S&P 500 returns after controlling for CPIAUCSL growth, or if triggered observations underperform the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth marks broad demand contraction and weaker earnings expectations, so a latest-vintage quarterly decline in GDPC1 predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: latest vintage GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth is below 0%
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15 the latest GDPC1 vintage available in the snapshot was 2022-01 with value 19727.918
failure_condition: Reject if latest-vintage negative GDPC1 growth is not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than positive GDPC1 growth in walk-forward tests, or if triggered negative signals have positive returns in more than 65% of cases.
